expectations.text: Controller class extends ControllerBase (not bare ContainerInjectionInterface)
expectations.evidence: ApiStatusController.php line 11: 'class ApiStatusController extends ControllerBase' -- correctly uses ControllerBase, not bare ContainerInjectionInterface.

expectations.text: Controller has static create() method accepting ContainerInterface
expectations.evidence: ApiStatusController.php line 43: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container)' -- proper factory method pulling config.factory and module_handler.

expectations.text: Constructor uses typed service parameters with DI (not \Drupal::service() calls in methods)
expectations.evidence: ApiStatusController.php lines 35-38: constructor accepts ConfigFactoryInterface and ModuleHandlerInterface, stores as properties. No \Drupal:: static calls anywhere in the class.

expectations.text: Route YAML uses _controller key with fully qualified class name
expectations.evidence: api_status_endpoint.routing.yml line 4: '_controller: \Drupal\api_status_endpoint\Controller\ApiStatusController::status' -- correct _controller key with FQCN.
api_status_endpoint.routing.yml line 7: '_access: TRUE' under requirements -- route is protected (public access explicitly declared, appropriate for status endpoint).
api_status_endpoint.info.yml line 4: 'core_version_requirement: ^10 || ^11' -- both D10 and D11 included.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en api_status_endpoint returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Transcript confirms module enabled successfully. Verified via ddev drush pm:list showing api_status_endpoint in enabled modules.

expectations.text: The API endpoint returns JSON: navigate to /api/system/status and verify page contains site name or module count data
expectations.evidence: E2E assertion PASS: page-contains 'site_name' at /api/system/status -- JSON response contains site_name field.
